description: Meta-repository containing only a Dockerfile for testing the external sdg_hub project from Red-Hat-AI-Innovation-Team

linting.notes: No linting configured. This repository contains no source code, only a Dockerfile.

testing.notes: This repository has no tests of its own. The Dockerfile builds a container that clones https://github.com/Red-Hat-AI-Innovation-Team/sdg_hub.git and runs its integration tests with 'python -m pytest tests/integration/ -v'. Tests require OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable.

ci.notes: No CI/CD configured. This is a manually-built container configuration repository.

build.notes: The only 'build' is building the container image itself. The container then clones an external repository and tests it.

conventions.notes: Not applicable - this repository contains no source code.

test_commands.notes: Cannot validate - requires OPENAI_API_KEY and tests external repository, not this one

container_recipe.notes: This Dockerfile does not test code in this repository. It clones and tests an external repository (Red-Hat-AI-Innovation-Team/sdg_hub). The sdg-hub repository itself contains only this Dockerfile, a README, and a LICENSE file.

results.output_snippet:
STEP 1/12: FROM quay.io/opendatahub/workbench-images:jupyter-minimal-ubi9-python-3.12-pr-2113
STEP 2/12: USER 0
STEP 3/12: RUN dnf install -y git && dnf clean all
STEP 4/12: USER 1001
STEP 5/12: WORKDIR /opt/app-root/src
STEP 6/12: ARG GIT_BRANCH=main
STEP 7/12: RUN git clone --branch ${GIT_BRANCH} --single-branch https://github.com/Red-Hat-AI-Innovation-Team/sdg_hub.git
STEP 8/12: ARG SDG_HUB_VERSION=latest
STEP 9/12: RUN pip install sdg_hub[examples,dev]
results.notes: Dockerfile is syntactically valid and builds successfully. Base image pulls, git is available, external repo clones, pip install begins.

validation.summary: Dockerfile builds successfully but cannot run full tests without OPENAI_API_KEY. Repository contains no code or tests of its own.

gaps: This repository contains no source code to lint or test
This repository has no CI/CD pipeline
The Dockerfile tests an external repository, not this one
Integration tests require OPENAI_API_KEY which prevents automated validation
No tests exist for the Dockerfile itself (e.g., container structure tests)
No pre-commit hooks or linting for the Dockerfile
No branch protection or CODEOWNERS configured
notes: This is not a traditional software repository. It's a meta-repository containing only a Dockerfile that builds a test container for an external project (Red-Hat-AI-Innovation-Team/sdg_hub). There is no code in this repository to lint, test, or validate. An AI agent cannot use this repository to validate patches because there is no code here - only a container build configuration. The repository exists solely to provide a containerized test environment for the upstream sdg_hub project.
